issue_title: DHCP Server of systemd-networkd will not work on USB Gadget RNDIS connected to Windows
issue_body: **systemd version the issue has been seen with** 249.5 **Used distribution** Buildroot **Linux kernel version used** (`uname -a`) `Linux smartcross 5.15.10 #8 SMP PREEMPT Sat Jan 22 20:15:20 CST 2022 aarch64 GNU/Linux` **CPU architecture issue was seen on** aarch64 **Expected behaviour you didn't see** systemd-networkd respond to DHCP requests sent from Windows **Unexpected behaviour you saw** systemd-networkd does not respond to DHCP requests sent from Windows. The reason is simple: in https://github.com/systemd/systemd/blob/f939a8984afb33e3242b8d18b40279876230dc61/src/libsystemd-network/sd-dhcp-server.c#L904 the DHCP server will reject anything but with `message->htype == ARPHRD_ETHER`(0x01). But for USB RNDIS interfaces Windows will send htype ARPHRD_ARCNET (0x07), causing networkd to drop this request. Maybe we can add an exception for ARPHRD_ARCNET here? **Steps to reproduce the problem** 1. Grab a RPi Zero or RPi 4 with USB gadget functionality. You can use RPi OS or any OS you like, as this doesn't affect the reproducibility of this issue. 2. Set up USB Gadget, like ```bash CONFIGFS_ROOT=/sys/kernel/config cd "${CONFIGFS_ROOT}"/usb_gadget mkdir g1 cd g1 echo 0x5678 > idVendor echo 0x1234 > idProduct mkdir strings/0x409 echo "my vendor" > strings/0x409/manufacturer echo "my device" > strings/0x409/product mkdir configs/c.1 mkdir functions/rndis.usb0 ln -s functions/rndis.usb0 configs/c.1 ls /sys/class/udc > UDC ``` 3. Add a systemd-networkd network file for the USB interface, like ``` [Match] Name=usb0 [Network] DHCPServer=true DHCP=no Address=10.222.11.1/24 [DHCPServer] PoolOffset=128 PoolSize=32 EmitRouter=false EmitNTP=false EmitDNS=false EmitTimezone=false ``` 4. Bring up the interface. systemd-networkd will not respond to DHCP request sent by Windows.
pr_title: dhcp-server: support packets from non-Ethernet interfaces
